name: Mathias Payer

work.note: His homepage, CV, and the ranking page all place him at EPFL.

tracking.note: Tracked as Qiang Liu's postdoctoral advisor; his CV also exposes many downstream student relationships.

sources.note: CV says he was a postdoctoral scholar in Dawn Song's BitBlaze group and earned Doctor of Science ETH in Computer Science with advisor Thomas R. Gross.
His homepage says he leads the HexHive research group at EPFL and was a postdoctoral researcher with Dawn Song at UC Berkeley.
The HexHive page lists current members, graduated Ph.D. students, and postdocs under Mathias Payer.

summary: EPFL HexHive professor. His CV says he earned his doctorate from ETH Zurich under Thomas R. Gross, later worked as Dawn Song's postdoc at UC Berkeley, and explicitly lists current and graduated students.

undergraduate.note: The CV exposes ETH Zurich diploma/master information, but this version does not force it into the undergraduate slot.

masters.note: The CV provides ETH Zurich diploma/master information, which is currently recorded as the master's stage.

phd.status: Doctor of Science ETH in Computer Science
phd.note: The CV education section says Doctor of Science ETH in Computer Science, Advisor: Thomas R. Gross.

postdoc.note: The CV says he was a postdoctoral scholar in Dawn Song's BitBlaze group at UC Berkeley from Sept. 2012 to July 2014.

name: Michael Backes

work.note: CISPA sources say he has been the founding director and chairman of CISPA since 2017; the ranking page also places him at CISPA.

tracking.note: Enriched from the imported ranking-page seed using official CISPA sources.

sources.note: The ranking page provides a DBLP author id and a CISPA affiliation for the imported seed.
The CISPA profile page says he has been the founding director and chairman of CISPA since 2017 and a professor at Saarland University since 2005.

The CISPA CV says he studied Computer Science and Mathematics at Saarland University, earned a Diploma in Computer Science in 2001, a Diploma in Mathematics in 2002, and a Ph.D. in Computer Science in 2002.

summary: CISPA founding director and Saarland University professor. Official CISPA sources say he studied Computer Science and Mathematics at Saarland University, earned a Computer Science diploma in 2001, a Mathematics diploma in 2002, and a Ph.D. in Computer Science in 2002.

undergraduate.note: The CISPA CV says he studied Computer Science and Mathematics at Saarland University.

masters.note: The CISPA CV says he earned a Diploma in Computer Science in 2001 and a Diploma in Mathematics in 2002 at Saarland University.

phd.note: The CISPA CV says he earned a Ph.D. in Computer Science in 2002 at Saarland University, but does not name an advisor.

postdoc.note: The current official sources list a researcher role at IBM Research Zurich after the Ph.D., but do not explicitly describe it as a postdoc.
